title: Employees Without Orders
description: Find all employees who have NOT placed any order. Return only the employee name.
reference_query: SELECT employees.name FROM employees LEFT JOIN orders ON employees.id = orders.employee_id WHERE orders.id IS NULL
hints: LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table, even if no match exists in the right table. Unmatched right-side columns are NULL.
After a LEFT JOIN, filter for rows where the right table's column IS NULL to find unmatched rows.
You can also use NOT IN with a subquery: WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT employee_id FROM orders)
explanation: LEFT JOIN + IS NULL is the classic pattern to find rows with no match in the related table. It is generally more efficient than NOT IN with a subquery.
